question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any assessment has been made on actions to increase the participation in autism and neurodiversity awareness training for health and social care staff, given that only 365 people have undertaken the training.
answer_text: Autism and neurodiversity awareness training for Health and Social Care (HSC) staff was formally launched, with my endorsement, in December 2025. Promotion has been through interface meetings with Assistant Directors and Heads of Service supported by Departmental and Trust social media channels. The training is also being promoted via HSC Trust Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) Coordinators and Quality and Inclusion Teams.The HSC workforce have a schedule of training to complete during working hours. This training is delivered in modular format, with a total completion time of approximately 3 hours. This allows staff to progress at their own pace, with a certificate of completion only issued once all modules have been successfully completed. My Department continues to monitor uptake on a monthly basis and has observed a month on month increase in completion. As of 31 March 2026, a total of 1,276 staff across HSC Trusts and Arms Length Bodies have started the training and have completed Session 1: Understanding the Autistic Experience in Healthcare. A total of 817 staff have completed the full training.My Department is committed to continuing to actively promote uptake of the training across HSC. We continue to monitor training uptake regionally to identify opportunities for targeted support or promotion of the training.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 30665/22-27, for an update on the Barrier Busting Taskforce regarding improving the mobile phone signal in rural areas
answer_text: The formal work of the Barrier Busting Taskforce (BBT) is nearing completion. Of the eight Mobile Action Plan (MAP) actions the BBT was tasked with delivering, four have been fully delivered, three have been largely completed with remaining elements being finalised by the responsible Departments/ organisations, and one action has been deemed unnecessary for the BBT. The work of the BBT will help to secure the fast and efficient roll out of mobile connectivity infrastructure here. It has established working processes and built relationships between key stakeholders that are now delivering real results on the ground that delivers better mobile connectivity. As part of the formal closure process of the BBT, I intend to write to the organisations to clearly identify the outstanding issues and will request that they keep abreast of progress on the remaining outstanding elements required to finalise the MAP actions.I remain committed to encouraging the rollout of mobile connectivity, recognising the continued service issues experienced, particularly in rural areas. My officials will continue to work with the mobile industry and other partners to ensure that citizens and businesses throughout the North can fully avail of the benefits that wireless technology can offer.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 35941/22-27, for an update on this work and the roundtable discussion convened by Road Victim Support Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The development of legislative provisions to prevent the unauthorised recording and sharing of photographs of road traffic collisions is a cross-departmental issue. Unfortunately, my Department does not have capacity or resources to take this issue forward during this mandate. However, as I explained at the roundtable discussion convened by Road Victim Support Northern Ireland, my Department can support the development of any such proposals if the Department for Infrastructure (which has lead policy responsibility for road safety matters), the Justice Committee, or an Assembly member wish to legislate on the matter during this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any assessment has been made of how his Department is working with the Independent Autism Reviewer, including any further improvements that could be made to support this work.
answer_text: In early 2025, a Head of Office was appointed to support the establishment of the Autism Reviewer’s Office and identify and agree key priorities for the role. Departmental officials continue to work with the Autism Reviewer.  Information-sharing processes have been streamlined and I understand that they are working well.  The need for further resource to support the effective operation of the Office will be kept under review.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will ensure there are measurable targets within the Autism Strategy 2023-2028's Delivery Plan.
answer_text: My Department has developed the next Delivery Plan for the Autism Strategy for the period 2025 to 2028.  As required in legislation, the Plan will include SMART targets and will clearly identify how success will be measured. The intention is to publish the Plan on the Department of Health website in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether any assessment has been made of the 2026 requirements for the Farm Sustainability Payment not jeopardising the long-term viability of small family farms in Less Favoured Areas that cannot meet the temporary crop criteria.
answer_text: There are no temporary crop requirements for the Farm Sustainability Payment (FSP) in 2026. 		To be eligible to claim FSP in 2026, farm businesses must have met the FSP Historic Years policy which required businesses formed before 1 January 2022 to have had at any time during the period 1 January 2020 to 31 December 2021: -	 a.	Kept cattle or sheep or reared goats registered on APHIS; or										b.	Reared deer for meat and sold that meat commercially; orc.		Kept poultry registered with the Department as a commercial flock or pigs registered on APHIS, combined with selling grass (not including the sale of agistment grazing rights); ord.		Farmed at least three hectares of a determined area with an arable or horticultural crop specified in Schedule 1 and declared in an application to the Department for either of those years; ore.		Been a contract rearer with a contract in place which demonstrated that they managed the livestock and associated grassland, bore the risks in relation to the agricultural activity being carried out and obtained benefits from it.		The FSP Historic Years policy was introduced to help deliver the policy intent of FSP which is to is to act as a balance between providing a safety net which will help a farm business withstand ‘shocks’ that are beyond its ability to manage effectively and encouraging farm businesses to become more environmentally sustainable, efficient and resilient. 		My Department wrote to all identified businesses impacted by this policy in June 2025 setting out the options open to them. 		The Historic Years policy only applies to eligibility for the 2026 FSP scheme year. Impacted businesses can acquire new entitlements and apply for FSP in 2027 or in subsequent years, providing they meet all other FSP eligibility requirements.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any consideration given to ensuring that the 2026 eligibility rules for the Farm Sustainability Payment will apply to active farm businesses whose land only supports grass production.
answer_text: Active farm businesses that have met the Farm Sustainability Payment (FSP) Historic Years policy, or were formed on or after 1 January 2022, and are producing only grass will be eligible to claim the Farm Sustainability Payment (FSP) in 2026 providing all other FSP eligibility criteria have been met.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) any consideration given to the administrative and financial implications for small family farms of being required to dispose of their entitlements in 2026 and reacquire them in 2027; and (ii) whether alternative arrangements were explored.
answer_text: The Farm Sustainability Payment (FSP) scheme is designed to improve the resilience of farm businesses and to act as a balance between providing a safety net which will help a farm business withstand ‘shocks’ that are beyond its ability to manage effectively and encouraging farm businesses to become more environmentally sustainable and efficient.		To help ensure that FSP is directed towards farm businesses undertaking agricultural activity, the FSP Historic Years policy required businesses formed before 1 January 2022 to have had at any time during the period 1 January 2020 to 31 December 2021: -	a.	Kept cattle or sheep or reared goats registered on APHIS; or										b.	Reared deer for meat and sold that meat commercially; orc.		Kept poultry registered with the Department as a commercial flock or pigs registered on APHIS, combined with selling grass (not including the sale of agistment grazing rights); ord.		Farmed at least three hectares of a determined area with an arable or horticultural crop specified in Schedule 1 and declared in an application to the Department for either of those years; ore.		Been a contract rearer with a contract in place which demonstrated that they managed the livestock and associated grassland, bore the risks in relation to the agricultural activity being carried out and obtained benefits from it.		My department wrote to all identified businesses impacted by this policy in June 2025, setting out the options open to them. This includes the option to sell their entitlements before they expire on 15 May 2026.		The Historic Years policy applies to eligibility for the 2026 FSP scheme year and impacted businesses can acquire new entitlements and apply for FSP in 2027, or in subsequent years, providing they meet all other FSP eligibility requirements.As in previous years the transfer of entitlements by gift, sale or lease using the online service is quick and simple to use and familiar to businesses and / or their agents.  The restrictions on transfers in 2026 are:transfers by lease are limited to one year. businesses deemed ineligible because of the historic years exercise cannot transfer directly to a business registered with the Department from 18 November 2025. transfers to a business which has yet to be assessed for the active farmer requirements.The only restriction remaining in 2027 will be in relation to the active farmer requirements, which is the case every year.The financial aspect (agreed price) of transferring entitlements is a matter for the transferor and transferee. The Department’s Entitlement Register merely records the business holding the entitlements on 15 May and the value of those entitlements for payment of the Farm Sustainability Payment.Due to the simplicity of the system, no alternative arrangements were explored.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 39657/22-27, to detail (i) the current arrangements in place; (ii) any issues posed to grieving families by the current arrangements, when it comes to fatal road traffic accidents; and (iii) whether she will write to her counterpart in the Irish Government on the issue.
answer_text: The detailed operational matters raised fall within the remit of the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) who are accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I would, therefore, direct the Member to the PSNI for further information. Should specific issues be identified that require intergovernmental engagement, I would give consideration to writing to my counterpart in the Irish Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Project Gigabit.
answer_text: The contract for Project Gigabit, worth £34.6m, was awarded to Fibrus Networks on 16 December 2025 and will provide full fibre infrastructure to 9,333 predominantly-rural premises here by 2027. At the end of January 2026, Fibrus reported that they completed work enabling nearly 900 of the 9,333 premises to order a fibre-based service, and are on track to make these services available to a further 200 premises by the end of this financial year. Already, it is estimated that 94% of all premises here can access gigabit-capable broadband – compared to 86% in Britain, and 88% in the rest of Ireland – and Project Gigabit will improve the availability of gigabit-capable services further.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 38925/22-27, whether his Department has a timeframe for the Planning Improvement Programme, to address the backlog of planning consultation responses.
answer_text: The NIEA Planning Improvement Plan sets out 22 strategic actions within 5 work strands. The timeframe for completing the final actions extends to September 2026.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the revitalisation of the Housing Executive.
answer_text: Progress continues to be made to resolve the borrowing issue that limits the NIHE’s ability to invest in its homes.In a significant step forward, the UK Government gave its commitment to address this issue when it confirmed in June 2025 that the treatment of NIHE borrowing will be considered as part of NI Fiscal Framework negotiations. I have personally raised this matter at every opportunity in discussions with UK Government, including with the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, under-Secretaries of State and the relevant Housing Minister. I am advised that my officials, alongside counterparts from the Department of Finance, most recently met with Northern Ireland Office and HM Treasury officials in December. This was a helpful and constructive meeting, where a number of important points were discussed and agreed with HM Treasury. Work continues to address these.I am continuing to press for this to be resolved immediately, either through an immediate, up-front agreement, or for early resolution within Fiscal Framework negotiations. Officials are actively engaging with the Department of Finance and HM Treasury on this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has engaged or would consider engaging with her counterpart in the Irish Government on the issue of exemptions for An Garda Síochána and PSNI officers crossing the border when liaising with the families of people who are victims in fatal road traffic accidents.
answer_text: I am advised that established cross-border arrangements are already in place, and the PSNI has not raised any concerns with me.  Any consideration of changes, including specific cross-border exemptions, would be informed by operational advice. If required, I would be open to raising the matter with the Minister for Justice, Home Affairs and Migration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any action his Department is taking to address the backlog in NI Environment Agency consultee responses to planning applications.
answer_text: In order to address the backlog of planning consultation responses, the Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) has developed and is implementing a Planning Improvement Plan.  This plan includes a range of measures to improve planning performance and service, and covers 5 strands: technical advice and guidance; caseload management; monitoring and reporting on performance; process and IT systems; and, communication and stakeholder engagement.NIEA continues to work in collaboration with the Department for Infrastructure cross-governmental Planning Statutory Consultee Forum which aims to drive forward continuous improvements in the planning system.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
